text.title: ai-research-tools Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-research-tools as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: No single AI tool covers all stages; agents that try to use one tool for everything produce shallow, biased results.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Assign explicit tools per stage; do not let agents default to Claude or ChatGPT for all stages.

failure-mode.description: Tool quality for market research degrades sharply outside English-language content.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Perplexity, SerpAPI, and Google Trends optimize for English signals; non-English research requires specialized regional tools.

failure-mode.description: Competitor analysis tools (Crayon, Klue) require 2-4 weeks of initial setup before producing useful signals; agents cannot bootstrap this.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These tools require human configuration of competitor entities. Agents cannot invent the competitor list.

failure-mode.description: Interview analysis tools (Looppanel, Dovetail) only operate on pre-uploaded transcripts — agents cannot conduct interviews.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These are transcript processors, not interview conductors. Transcripts must exist before the tool can be called.

failure-mode.description: AI tools reflect the training cutoff or crawl date of their underlying data; freshness of results must be explicitly verified.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Perplexity crawls 2026 web data; older AI LLMs have 2024 cutoffs. Do not use tools without knowing their data freshness date.

failure-mode.description: Agents default to Claude or ChatGPT for all stages when not given explicit tool assignments.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: General-purpose LLMs produce citation-poor results for competitive and market data stages. Stage-to-tool assignments must be explicit in the orchestrator prompt, not inferred.

failure-mode.description: Rate limit errors from one tool propagate silently unless the orchestrator handles exceptions per tool.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without per-tool exception handling, a rate-limited stage produces empty output that looks like "no data", corrupting the synthesis and producing gaps invisible to the human reviewer.

failure-mode.description: Tool outputs have different schemas; the synthesis step receives inconsistent data unless normalized.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Perplexity → citations as URLs; SerpAPI → structured JSON; Google Trends → DataFrames. The orchestrator must normalize before the synthesis call, not after.

failure-mode.description: "AI tool" in 2026 means both AI-native tools (Perplexity, Elicit) and legacy tools with AI features bolted on (Statista AI, SurveyMonkey AI); their reliability for agent pipelines differs significantly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always include the API reference in the agent's context for each tool. Agents will hallucinate capabilities for unfamiliar tools if given only the tool name.
